questions.text: Why does the Surah begin with glorifying Allah’s name?
questions.answer: To center the heart on Allah’s greatness before discussing guidance, reminders, and accountability.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that nature is random and not a sign of Allah.
questions.answer: False. It points to creation, proportion, guidance, and provision as signs.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach by mentioning pasture turning into dark stubble?
questions.answer: Worldly beauty fades; life changes quickly, so attach the heart to Allah and the Hereafter.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-A'la teach confidence in the Quran’s preservation?
questions.answer: It reassures the Prophet about recitation and that Allah ensures what is needed remains.

questions.text: What does Allah’s knowledge of what is declared and hidden teach about sincerity?
questions.answer: Allah knows intentions; worship and morality must be sincere, not performative.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests reminders are useless and should be abandoned.
questions.answer: False. It teaches giving reminders when they benefit and that the God-fearing accept them.

questions.text: Who benefits most from reminders according to this Surah’s message?
questions.answer: Those who fear Allah and want guidance.

questions.text: What is one sign of the ‘most wretched’ person described in this Surah?
questions.answer: They avoid reminders and persist in rejection, leading to punishment.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the Great Fire and its misery?
questions.answer: It describes a torment where a person neither truly lives nor finds relief, warning against denial.

questions.text: What does 'purifying oneself' (tazakka) include in a practical sense?
questions.answer: Leaving shirk and sins, improving character, and sincerely returning to Allah.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the relationship between dhikr and prayer?
questions.answer: True success includes remembering Allah and establishing prayer as lived practice.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that worldly life is better because it is immediate.
questions.answer: False. It teaches the Hereafter is better and more enduring.

questions.text: How can someone apply the Surah’s message about preferring dunya?
questions.answer: Notice where dunya pulls you away from prayer or ethics, then choose the Hereafter consistently.

questions.text: What does it mean that these teachings exist in earlier scriptures?
questions.answer: Core guidance—tawhid, purification, and accountability—was taught by earlier prophets too.

questions.text: What is one lesson from mentioning the scriptures of Ibrahim and Musa?
questions.answer: Islam is continuous with earlier prophetic teachings and calls to the same fundamentals.

questions.text: How can this Surah help someone struggling with spiritual consistency?
questions.answer: It emphasizes simple pillars: glorify Allah, accept reminders, purify the self, and pray.

questions.text: What is one daily practice inspired by the Surah’s opening command?
questions.answer: Begin the day with tasbih and gratitude, then protect the prayers on time.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-A'la balance hope and fear?
questions.answer: It highlights the path of purification and prayer (hope) and warns of the wretched and the Fire (fear).

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that purification is only physical cleanliness.
questions.answer: False. It includes spiritual purification from shirk, sin, and bad character.

questions.text: Use its themes—glorify Allah, accept reminders, purify yourself, prefer the Hereafter—to guide daily choices.
questions.answer: Use its themes—glorify Allah, accept reminders, purify yourself, prefer the Hereafter—to guide daily choices.

questions.text: What is the opening command of Surah Al-A'la?
questions.answer: Exalt the name of your Lord, the Most High.

questions.text: What four actions of Allah are mentioned in this Surah's opening section?
questions.answer: He created and proportioned, ordained and guided, and brought out the pasture.

questions.text: What happens to the pasture (vegetation) eventually?
questions.answer: Allah makes it 'blackened stubble' (Ghuthā'an ahwā).

questions.text: What promise does Allah give the Prophet (SAW) regarding the Quran?
questions.answer: We will make you recite, [O Muhammad], and you will not forget.

questions.text: Is there an exception to the Prophet not forgetting the Quran?
questions.answer: Yes, except what Allah wills.

questions.text: Does Allah know what is declared and what is hidden?
questions.answer: Yes, 'He knows what is declared and what is hidden'.

questions.text: What will Allah ease for the Prophet (SAW)?
questions.answer: He will ease him toward the 'easy way' (Islam and its laws).

questions.text: When should the Prophet give a reminder?
questions.answer: If the reminder benefits (the people).

questions.text: Who will be reminded by the Prophet's message?
questions.answer: He who fears Allah.

questions.text: Who will avoid the reminder?
questions.answer: The 'most wretched' (Al-Ashqa).

questions.text: What is the fate of the wretched one?
questions.answer: He will enter the 'Great Fire' (An-Nar al-Kubra).

questions.text: Will the person in the Great Fire die or live?
questions.answer: He will neither die therein nor live (meaning a state of constant agony).

questions.text: Who is the one who has succeeded in this Surah?
questions.answer: He who has purified himself.

questions.text: What two things does the successful person do in this Surah?
questions.answer: Remembers the name of his Lord and prays.

questions.text: What do people generally prefer according to this Surah?
questions.answer: The life of this world.

questions.text: How is the Hereafter compared to the worldly life?
questions.answer: The Hereafter is better and more enduring (Khayrun wa Abqa).

questions.text: Were these teachings mentioned in previous scriptures?
questions.answer: Yes, they are mentioned in the former scriptures.

questions.text: Name the two Prophets whose scriptures are specifically mentioned at the end.
questions.answer: The scriptures of Ibrahim and Musa.

questions.text: What is the virtue of reciting this Surah in Friday and Eid prayers?
questions.answer: It was the Sunnah of the Prophet (SAW) to recite it frequently in these prayers.

questions.text: What is the meaning of 'Tazakka' in this Surah?
questions.answer: Purifying oneself from shirk, sins, and bad character.
